description_long: This axe features a robust, roughly hewn wooden handle with three distinct grip segments, each separated by slightly raised bands, giving it a hand-crafted appearance. The handle is cylindrical and straight, with a natural wood texture and visible grain patterns. The axe head is large and made of a stone-like material, shaped into a broad, triangular blade with a single sharp edge and a pointed tip. The blade is securely mounted perpendicular to the handle, with the mounting area appearing thick and reinforced. The overall design suggests a primitive or rustic tool, possibly inspired by early hand axes or fantasy settings. The materials and construction evoke durability and a utilitarian function, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks.
description: A rustic axe with a thick, wooden handle and a large, triangular stone blade, featuring a hand-crafted, primitive design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, triangular stone blade pointing right.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the cylindrical handle, with the blade edge barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the blade pointing left, mirroring view 0 and showing the same handle and blade features.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view displaying the pointed tip of the blade and the end of the handle, emphasizing the thickness of the mounting.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with three raised grip bands, natural wood texture, and a round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Large, triangular stone blade with a single sharp edge and pointed tip, no beard or poll, primitive and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade inserted through the handle and reinforced by thick bands.
